# Project Roadmap Template

## Document Control
- **Project/program name:**
- **Document version:**
- **Prepared by:**
- **Preparation date:**
- **Last updated by:**
- **Last revision date:**

## Purpose
Provide a high-level view of the project lifecycle from initiation through transition to operations,
including phases, milestones, deliverables, and governance decision points. The roadmap aligns
stakeholders on scope, timeline, and readiness for operational handover.

## Audience
Project Sponsor, Project Manager, PMO, Delivery Leads, Operations, Key Stakeholders.

## Inputs
- Project charter (`docs/templates/governance/project-charter-waterfall.md`).
- Project management plan (`docs/templates/governance/project-management-plan-cross.md`).
- Schedule management plan (`docs/templates/schedule/schedule-management-plan-template-cross.md`).
- Risk management plan (`docs/templates/risk/risk-management-plan-template-cross.md`).

---

## 1. Project Overview

### 1.1 Project Description
Provide a concise summary of the project purpose, objectives, and strategic value.

### 1.2 Scope Summary
- **In scope:**
- **Out of scope:**

### 1.3 Key Parameters
| Parameter | Value | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Start date |  |  |
| Target end date |  |  |
| Delivery approach | [Phased/Big Bang/Hybrid] |  |
| Key dependencies |  |  |
| Key constraints |  |  |

---

## 2. Project Phases Overview

| Phase | Duration | Start | End | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Initiation |  |  |  | Charter, governance setup |
| Planning |  |  |  | Requirements, plans, baseline |
| Execution - Design |  |  |  | Solution design, architecture |
| Execution - Build |  |  |  | Configuration, development |
| Execution - Test |  |  |  | Integration, UAT, performance |
| Implementation |  |  |  | Cutover, training, go-live |
| Hypercare |  |  |  | Stabilization support |
| Transition to Operations |  |  |  | Handover, documentation |
| Operations | Ongoing |  |  | Steady-state support |

### 2.1 Phase Entry and Exit Criteria
Define the entry and exit criteria for each phase.

**Example: Planning Phase**
- **Entry:** Approved charter, core team assigned, kickoff complete.
- **Exit:** Management plan approved, requirements signed off, baseline schedule set.

---

## 3. Key Activities and Deliverables by Phase

### Initiation
**Activities**
- Develop charter and governance model
- Identify stakeholders and success measures

**Deliverables**
- Project charter
- Stakeholder register
- Initial risk assessment

### Planning
**Activities**
- Requirements definition
- Schedule and budget baselining
- Change and communications planning

**Deliverables**
- Project management plan
- Requirements specification
- Schedule baseline
- Risk management plan

### Execution - Design
**Activities**
- Solution architecture and design
- Integration and data migration planning

**Deliverables**
- Solution design documentation
- Integration design
- Test strategy

### Execution - Build
**Activities**
- Configuration and development
- Integration build
- Test case development

**Deliverables**
- Configured solution
- Integration components
- Test cases

### Execution - Test
**Activities**
- System and integration testing
- UAT execution

**Deliverables**
- Test results and defect logs
- UAT sign-off

### Implementation
**Activities**
- Cutover planning and readiness checks
- Training and go-live

**Deliverables**
- Cutover plan
- Training completion records
- Go-live approval

### Hypercare
**Activities**
- Intensive support and monitoring
- Issue triage and stabilization

**Deliverables**
- Hypercare status reports
- Issue resolution log

### Transition to Operations
**Activities**
- Knowledge transfer
- Operational readiness assessment

**Deliverables**
- Handover documentation
- Operational readiness sign-off

---

## 4. Major Milestones and Decision Gates

| Milestone/Gate | Target Date | Description | Decision Makers | Key Deliverables |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Project approval |  |  |  | Business case, charter |
| Planning complete |  |  |  | Management plan, schedule |
| Design approval |  |  |  | Design documents |
| Build complete |  |  |  | Configured solution, test readiness |
| UAT sign-off |  |  |  | UAT results |
| Go-live readiness |  |  |  | Cutover plan, risk assessment |
| Go-live |  |  |  | Deployment checklist |
| Hypercare exit |  |  |  | Stabilization metrics |
| Transition complete |  |  |  | Handover checklist |
| Project closure |  |  |  | Closure report |

### 4.1 Decision Gate Criteria
List the criteria required to pass each decision gate (e.g., no critical defects, training
completion, operational readiness).

---

## 5. Transition Activities

### 5.1 Transition Strategy
Describe the approach to handover from project to operations, including timing and overlap.

### 5.2 Transition Timeline
| Activity | Start | End | Responsibility | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Knowledge transfer sessions |  |  |  |  |
| Documentation finalization |  |  |  |  |
| Shadow support |  |  |  |  |
| Formal handover |  |  |  |  |

### 5.3 Knowledge Transfer Checklist
- [ ] System architecture and configuration
- [ ] Integration points and dependencies
- [ ] Monitoring and alerting procedures
- [ ] Backup and recovery procedures
- [ ] Support workflows and SLAs

---

## 6. Hypercare Period

### 6.1 Hypercare Scope and Duration
Define the hypercare length, coverage hours, and modules/processes in scope.

### 6.2 Support Model
| Support Level | Scope | Contact/Owner | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Level 1 | User support |  |  |
| Level 2 | Functional support |  |  |
| Level 3 | Technical/vendor support |  |  |

### 6.3 Hypercare KPIs
| Metric | Target | Measurement | Frequency |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| System availability |  |  |  |
| Critical incidents |  |  |  |
| Issue resolution time |  |  |  |
| User satisfaction |  |  |  |

---

## 7. Operational Readiness

### 7.1 Readiness Assessment Areas
- **People:** staffing, training, roles
- **Process:** incident/change management, SLAs
- **Technology:** monitoring, backup, security
- **Documentation:** runbooks, guides, known issues

### 7.2 Readiness Checklist
| Area | Checklist Item | Status | Owner | Target Date | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| People | Support team trained |  |  |  |  |
| Process | Incident management in place |  |  |  |  |
| Technology | Monitoring configured |  |  |  |  |
| Documentation | Admin guide complete |  |  |  |  |

---

## 8. Post-Implementation Support Model

### 8.1 Support Organization
- **Tier 1:** Help desk
- **Tier 2:** Application support
- **Tier 3:** Technical support
- **External:** Vendor support

### 8.2 Governance
| Decision Type | Owner | Approval Body | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Enhancement requests |  |  |  |
| Architectural changes |  |  |  |
| Release scheduling |  |  |  |

### 8.3 Continuous Improvement
Describe how enhancements are requested, prioritized, and delivered post go-live.
